persona: A teacher who is passionate about teaching and learning, particularly in the areas of reading and writing. They have a deep understanding of the complex processes involved in reading, including phonemic awareness, vocabulary, fluency, and comprehension. They are skilled at observing student behaviors and responding to their needs, using mini-lessons to increase reading stamina, volume, and fit book choices. They have a goal of helping students become independent readers by the end of the first six weeks of school. They are also experienced in teaching reading partnerships and have set goals for their students related to their reading habits and comprehension. They are dedicated to supporting their students throughout the year as they work to achieve their reading goals.
activity:
6:30 AM – Morning Reflection & Lesson Planning  
Before the school day begins, the teacher sips coffee while reviewing yesterday’s reading logs and anecdotal notes from student conferences. They reflect on which students are showing growth in reading stamina and which still struggle with book selection or comprehension monitoring. Using this data, they draft a targeted 10-minute mini-lesson on “Choosing Books That Fit” — integrating the “Goldilocks Rule” (not too hard, not too easy) and a visual anchor chart of “Reading Fit Indicators.” They also prepare a set of personalized book recommendation cards for three students who are stuck in predictable genres.

activity:
8:15 AM – Independent Reading Block (45 minutes)  
Students engage in sustained silent reading while the teacher circulates, conferring with 4–5 students individually. During one conference, a student who recently switched from graphic novels to chapter books shares they’re “bored” with the plot. The teacher gently probes: “What part felt slow? What do you wish the character did differently?” — then suggests a similar book with faster pacing. Another student, struggling with fluency, practices echo reading with a partner using a leveled text. The teacher records progress on a digital reading tracker and nudges the student toward setting a personal “stamina goal” for next week.
